title: Rocket Lab launches 10th Synspective satellite
summary: Rocket Lab launched a radar-imaging satellite for Japanese company Synspective on June 26, marking its 10th such mission. The launch was delayed due to a previous responsive space mission.

title: Spain-backed fund joins FOSSA’s sovereign satellite communications push
summary: Spanish startup FOSSA Systems raised $10.5 million to expand its satellite connectivity constellation, with funding from a Spain-backed technology investment vehicle.

title: Starlink veterans launch startup to broaden megaconstellation ownership
summary: Former SpaceX engineers who worked on Starlink have launched a new startup to provide megaconstellation services for governments and companies seeking greater control over space-based infrastructure.

title: ISS repair spacewalk highlights concerns about station health
summary: NASA astronauts are preparing for a spacewalk to repair the ISS's robotic arm while advisers express concerns about the station's long-term health and spacesuit reliability. The repair highlights ongoing maintenance challenges for the aging orbital outpost.

title: Europe’s next security challenge is in orbit
summary: Europe faces a security challenge in space as satellite navigation underpins over 10% of its GDP. The reliance on satellite-dependent activities highlights the growing importance of space infrastructure.

title: Rocket Lab wins NASA award for three Electron launches
summary: NASA selected Rocket Lab to launch two science missions using three Electron rockets in 2027. This award highlights Rocket Lab's growing role in supporting NASA's scientific exploration efforts.

title: Weather Stream releases first light imagery from GEMS2 microwave radiometer
summary: Weather Stream has released the first light imagery from its GEMS2 microwave radiometer aboard the GEMS2-Amethyst satellite. The company, based in Boulder, Colorado, is now collecting global atmospheric observations.

title: Space Force’s new MUOS satellites to use Boeing’s 702MP spacecraft platform
summary: The U.S. Space Force will deploy new MUOS satellites using Boeing's 702MP spacecraft platform, which has previously supported commercial and military missions.

title: Botswana signs the Artemis Accords
summary: Botswana has signed the Artemis Accords, joining the international framework for responsible space exploration. This marks the country's commitment to peaceful and sustainable space activities.

title: Small satellite operators confront a bottleneck to space access
summary: Small satellite operators face challenges accessing space due to reliance on SpaceX's rideshare missions, which have become a bottleneck. The dependency on SpaceX's Falcon 9 Transporter and Bandwagon missions highlights a growing issue in the industry.

title: SpaceX sends 24 Starlink satellites into Earth orbit on Falcon 9 launch from California (video)
summary: SpaceX launched 24 Starlink satellites into Earth orbit using a Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California. This mission marks another deployment in the company's expanding satellite internet constellation.

title: 'Superman Returns' at 20: Is it a sequel? Is it a reboot? Two decades on, we're still not sure
summary: The article discusses the 20th anniversary of 'Superman Returns', questioning whether it was a sequel or reboot. It highlights Brandon Routh's role and the film's homage to the original Superman movie by Richard Donner.

title: Japanese probe set for super-close flyby on July 5: 'We're going to discover another beast to put in the zoo of asteroids'
summary: The Japanese Hayabusa2 probe is set for a super-close flyby of an asteroid on July 5, following its successful sample return mission from Ryugu. This maneuver is one of the closest and riskiest flybys in spaceflight history.

title: Watch China's Tiangong space station cross the moon in stunning amateur footage
summary: Amateur footage captures China's Tiangong space station transiting the moon near Tycho Crater. The video showcases the station's passage in a visually striking manner.

title: Watch SpaceX launch 15,000-pound SiriusXM satellite to orbit tonight
summary: SpaceX is set to launch a 15,000-pound SiriusXM satellite from Florida's Space Coast on June 28, with live coverage available. This mission highlights SpaceX's role in deploying large commercial satellites.

title: Could future astronauts use oysters as water filters? Here's why one company thinks so
summary: A company is developing a prototype oyster habitat for water filtration with the potential for future space missions. The early-stage prototype was publicly demonstrated earlier this year.

title: 'Logan's Run' at 50: Remembering this disco-age sci-fi classic on its golden anniversary
summary: The article reflects on the 50th anniversary of the sci-fi classic 'Logan's Run', highlighting its cultural impact during the disco era. It playfully references the film's iconic life-clock concept.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 216 — Dark Matter Intelligence
summary: Episode 216 of the 'This Week In Space' podcast features a discussion with physicist Dr. Daniel Whiteson about the search for extraterrestrial intelligences beyond human scope.

title: SpaceCamp at 40: A wish-fulfillment fantasy brought down to earth by NASA's real-life disaster
summary: The article discusses the impact of the Challenger disaster on the film 'SpaceCamp', which was released shortly after the tragedy and failed to resonate with audiences. The film, once a popular wish-fulfillment fantasy, was overshadowed by real-life spaceflight tragedies.

title: Blue Origin plans to fly New Glenn rocket again this year despite massive rocket explosion (video)
summary: Blue Origin aims to relaunch its New Glenn rocket this year following a massive explosion, with CEO Dave Limp setting an ambitious goal to complete repairs and have another rocket ready by the end of 2026.

title: NASA Announces Winners for 2026 Human Lander Challenge
summary: NASA revealed the winners of the 2026 Human Lander Challenge, honoring student-developed solutions for life support systems in lunar landers. The competition supports the Artemis program, aiming to return astronauts to the Moon.

title: NASA Tests New Refuel Device for Future In-Space Refueling Missions
summary: NASA is testing a new refueling device for future in-space missions, enabling spacecraft to refuel in Earth orbit before deep space exploration. This technology could be crucial for extended missions beyond low Earth orbit.

title: Partners, NASA Ready for June Launch of Swift Boost Mission
summary: NASA and partners are preparing for the June 30 launch of the Swift Boost Mission to raise the orbit of NASA's Swift observatory. This mission aims to extend the operational lifespan of the observatory.

title: NASA Identifies More Than 40 Space Technologies for Collaboration
summary: NASA selected 41 proposals from 37 companies to advance space technologies supporting long-term Moon presence and Mars exploration. These technologies focus on space transportation, planetary surface operations, and lunar capabilities.

title: Euclid Sees Heart of Milky Way
summary: Euclid, an ESA mission with NASA contributions, captured a new image of the heart of the Milky Way galaxy. This observation aligns with a region that NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope will study after its upcoming launch.

title: Bringing Signals to NASA
summary: Eric Fernandez shares his childhood fascination with rocket launches on the central California coast, inspired by watching them with his father and grandparents' service at Vandenberg Air Force Base.

title: NASA’s PACE Mission Studies Smoke, Fires
summary: NASA's PACE satellite is monitoring vegetation precursors to fires and smoke plumes during the North American fire season to improve wildfire understanding. The mission's data will help scientists analyze wildfire patterns and their environmental impacts.

title: Hubble Spies Starry Chandelier
summary: The NASA/ESA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the globular cluster NGC 6723, also known as the Chandelier Cluster. This cluster is a dense collection of stars that provides insights into stellar evolution and the early universe.

title: Expedition 73 Crew Reflects on Science, Teamwork, and Life in Orbit
summary: Astronauts and cosmonauts from Expedition 73 shared stories from their ISS missions at Space Center Houston, highlighting teamwork and ground support. The welcome home ceremony included members from NASA’s SpaceX Crew-10, Soyuz MS-27, and NASA’s SpaceX Crew-11 missions.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid space telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, providing data to support future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The findings highlight Euclid's capabilities in studying galactic structures.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX completed a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Booster 20 and Pad 2 are undergoing new work.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour is now displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. The shuttle, which completed 25 flights, will serve an educational mission.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week will feature six orbital launches globally, including the first flight of the Pegasus XL rocket alongside missions by Falcon 9 and Chinese launchers. The Pegasus XL launch is highlighted as a notable event in the upcoming spaceflight schedule.

title: SpaceX tests upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX conducted a demonstration mission for its upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle using a Falcon 9 rocket. This test marks a significant step in developing reusable reentry technology.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is planning repairs for the Canadarm2 as the CRS-34 mission departs. This follows a busy month of activities aboard the space station.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space is advancing toward the 2026 debut of its Terran R heavy-lift rocket, with the second stage now headed to Stennis Space Center. This marks a key milestone in the development of the fully reusable launch vehicle.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten launches are scheduled worldwide in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for the space industry. The launches will take place from various sites across the globe.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider, canceled the second launch of its Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays. The launch was scheduled by the company based in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. Flight 12 was completed about three weeks prior.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on space industry news, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting upcoming industry events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across its programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications, highlighting its potential versatility and capabilities. This innovation could significantly impact future satellite servicing and space station operations.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports European space science activities by involving research institutes.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing materials to withstand the harsh space environment. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is supported by these innovative minds.

title: SpaceX Alum Have Raised $9.2B, New Data Shows
summary: Former SpaceX employees have collectively raised $9.2 billion in funding for their new ventures. SpaceX has served as a talent incubator, producing engineers who later founded or joined other significant companies.

title: Firefly Acquires Autonomous Space Ops Company
summary: Firefly Aerospace acquired Space-ng, an AI-powered autonomous guidance systems provider. The acquisition aims to enhance Firefly's space operations capabilities.

title: Europe Expands its Launch Opportunities to New Companies
summary: Europe is increasing launch opportunities for new companies to boost its space industry demand. This expansion aims to foster growth and competition in the European launch sector.

title: Exclusive: SpaceComputer and Spacemanic Partner on Security Demo Mission
summary: SpaceComputer and Spacemanic have partnered to conduct a security demonstration mission to address hacking threats and shared-compute modules on rideshare satellites. Space Fabric, SpaceComputer's solution, aims to enhance on-orbit security.

title: Japan Eyes Sovereign D2D Satellite Network
summary: Japan plans to select a proposal for its domestically owned and operated D2D satellite network, called J-LEO. This initiative aims to enhance sovereign satellite communication capabilities.

title: Satellite Operators Launch Trade Association
summary: Amazon, Globalstar, Iridium, and Canada’s Telesat have formed the SpaceConnect Association, a new trade group for non-geostationary orbit (NGSO) satellite operators. This collaboration aims to advocate for policies and industry interests among NGSO satellite providers.

title: SpaceX Starfall Demo Provides Validation for Reentry Startups
summary: SpaceX launched a demo mission of its Starfall reentry capsule, marking a significant step in the emerging reentry sector. The event highlights growing competition and innovation in space reentry technologies.

title: NASA Watchdog: Launch Infrastructure Nearing Capacity
summary: NASA's launch facilities are expected to reach capacity by the end of the decade due to a surge in commercial launches, according to a report from the agency's inspector general.

title: Sophia Space Raises $7M, Taps Apex for 2027 Demo Flight
summary: Sophia Space raised $7 million and partnered with Apex for a 2027 demo flight to deploy edge compute hardware in orbit.

title: Commercial Markets Are Ready for EO — But the Understanding Gap Persists
summary: The EO Summit in London highlighted a gap in understanding between Earth Observation (EO) solutions and potential commercial customers. Experts emphasized the need to better communicate the value and applications of EO technologies to drive market adoption.

title: Calian to Acquire Canadian Service Provider Galaxy Broadband
summary: Calian Group is set to acquire Galaxy Broadband Communications for up to $52 million CAD ($36.6 million USD), expanding its satellite communications and remote connectivity solutions. The deal marks a strategic move in the Canadian space industry.

title: Tototheo Global to Deploy SES FlexMaritime in Expanded Maritime Deal
summary: Tototheo Global has expanded its agreement with SES to deploy the FlexMaritime connectivity service for the maritime market. The service combines SES’s satellite network with integrated solutions.

title: ReOrbit Secures Finnish Funding for ‘OrbitCloud’ R&D Program
summary: ReOrbit has secured 4.6 million euro ($5.3 million) in Finnish funding for its 'OrbitCloud' R&D program aimed at developing a space-based edge computing network. The initiative is supported by Business Finland.

title: AiRanaculus to Advance Networking Technologies for NASA Lunar and Space Communications Networks
summary: AiRanaculus secured a $5 million NASA contract to develop networking technologies for lunar and space communications networks. The funding will support advancements in NASA's space communication infrastructure.

title: Infleqtion Teams With Voyager in New Quantum Space Initiative
summary: Infleqtion, Voyager Technologies, Monarch Quantum, Armada, and the University of Colorado Boulder have partnered to advance U.S. quantum space initiatives. This collaboration aims to push the boundaries of quantum technology in space applications.

title: Pegasus Launch Set to Deploy Servicer Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift Observatory
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus rocket is scheduled to launch a servicing spacecraft to reboost NASA’s Swift Observatory before it re-enters Earth’s atmosphere. The mission aims to extend the observatory's operational life.

title: Mitsubishi Electric Contracts MDA Space for Digital Payload for Japanese Defense Satellite
summary: Mitsubishi Electric Corporation has awarded MDA Space a contract to develop a digital payload, antennas, and related components for a next-generation Japanese defense communications satellite. This collaboration highlights MDA Space's role in advancing Japan's defense satellite capabilities.

title: Capella Validates Mynaric Optical Terminal on Latest SAR Satellite
summary: Capella Space validated a Mynaric optical communications terminal on its latest SAR satellite, marking the first deployment of an optical terminal by Capella. The company also released the first synthetic aperture radar images from the satellite.

title: BAE Systems to Build Buses for Vantor’s Vantage Imaging Satellites
summary: BAE Systems will construct satellite buses for Vantor's Vantage imaging satellites, with the first two expected to be operational by 2029.
